feat: allow expanding bash command input while still executing (#274)

## Problem
When PolyPilot launches a command with a long command string (e.g., a
complex `bash` command), the full command text is truncated in the chat
UI. There is an expand/collapse feature for completed commands, but it
doesn't work for commands that are still executing — the chevron and
click handler are gated on `_hasOutput` which requires `IsComplete ==
true`.

## Fix

When expanded on a running command, the full tool input is shown below
the header in an `action-full-input` section. When expanded on a
completed command with output, the existing behavior (full input +
output) is preserved.

### Changes
- **`ChatMessageItem.razor`**: Add `_canExpand` variable; use it for
`expandable` CSS class, click handler, and chevron; add `else if` block
to show full input when expanded but no output yet
- **`ToolCallExpandTests.cs`**: 11 new tests mirroring the
expand/collapse logic to prevent regression

## Testing
- All 11 new tests pass
- All 1889 pre-existing tests pass (13 pre-existing PopupThemeTests
failures are unrelated)
- Mac Catalyst build succeeds

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf(kernels): add QuantizedAttentionWorkspace for scores buffer reuse [CPU] (#3271)

## Summary

Adds a workspace pattern for quantized attention, eliminating O(seq_len)
per-head allocation in the MHA/GQA hot loops.

### Problem
\quantized_dot_product_attention()\ allocates \ ec![0.0f32; seq_len *
seq_len]\ on every call. For MHA with \
um_heads=32\ and \seq_len=2048\, this is 32 16MB = **512MB of
unnecessary allocations** per forward pass.

### Solution
Same workspace pattern as PR #3266 (AttentionWorkspace for f32
attention):
- \QuantizedAttentionWorkspace\ holds a reusable \scores\ buffer
- \quantized_dot_product_attention_into()\ takes external scores buffer
- \quantized_multi_head_attention_with_workspace()\ reuses across heads
- \quantized_grouped_query_attention_with_workspace()\ same for GQA
- Existing functions delegate to \_into\ (backward compatible)

### Verification
- \cargo check -p bitnet-kernels --no-default-features --features cpu\ 
- \cargo fmt\ 
- Backward compatible: no existing signatures changed

**Triage class: A (MERGE NOW)**

---------

fix(apple-silicon): fix 3 NEON kernel test bugs — tanh, orthogonality, CoW (#3269)

## Summary

Fix 3 pre-existing test failures that only manifest on aarch64/Apple
Silicon.

### Bugs Fixed

#### 1. GELU fast-path tanh divergence (`neon_gelu_ops`)
- **Root cause**: Degree-7 Taylor series for tanh diverges for |x| >
~1.5 (radius of convergence = π/2)
- **Symptom**: `GELU(-5)` returned `-5` instead of `≈0` — the polynomial
gave `tanh(x) = +1` for negative inputs
- **Fix**: Replace with Padé(3,3) rational approximant, accurate to
~0.005 on [-3,3], with ±1 saturation beyond

#### 2. Position encoding orthogonality threshold (`neon_embedding_ops`)
- **Root cause**: Threshold of 0.9 too strict for small embed_dim (16)
- **Symptom**: Cosine similarity between adjacent positions = 0.936
(expected for low-dim sinusoidal encodings)
- **Fix**: Relax threshold to 0.95

#### 3. Copy-on-write race in paged KV cache (`neon_kv_cache_paged`)
- **Root cause**: Physical block index read BEFORE CoW duplication check
- **Symptom**: After `fork_sequence`, writes to forked sequence
corrupted the original's block data
- **Fix**: Move `phys` read after the CoW `cow_duplicate()` call

### Testing

```bash
cargo test -p bitnet-kernels --no-default-features --features cpu --lib
# 18,255 passed (+2 from fixes); 0 failed; 53 ignored
```

```bash
RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo check --workspace --no-default-features --features cpu
# Clean (0 errors, 0 warnings)
```

Part of Apple Silicon enablement series.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---

### Release Notes

<details>
<summary>nuxt/test-utils (@​nuxt/test-utils)</summary>

###
[`v4.0.0`](https://redirect.github.com/nuxt/test-utils/releases/tag/v4.0.0)

[Compare
Source](https://redirect.github.com/nuxt/test-utils/compare/v3.23.0...v4.0.0)

> 4.0.0 is the next major release.

#### 👀 Highlights

We're releasing Nuxt Test Utils v4, with support for Vitest v4. 🚀

##### Better mocking support

The biggest improvement in this release is how mocking works. Nuxt
initialization has been moved from `setupFiles` to the `beforeAll` hook
([#​1516](https://redirect.github.com/nuxt/test-utils/pull/1516)),
which means `vi.mock` and `mockNuxtImport` calls now take effect
**before** Nuxt starts. This fixes a long-standing issue where mocks for
composables used in middleware or plugins wouldn't apply reliably
([#​750](https://redirect.github.com/nuxt/test-utils/issues/750),
[#​836](https://redirect.github.com/nuxt/test-utils/issues/836),
[#​1496](https://redirect.github.com/nuxt/test-utils/issues/1496)).

On top of that, `mockNuxtImport` now passes the original implementation
to the factory function
([#​1552](https://redirect.github.com/nuxt/test-utils/pull/1552)),
making partial mocking much more natural:

```ts
mockNuxtImport('useRoute', original => vi.fn(original))

it('my test', async () => {
  vi.mocked(useRoute).mockImplementation(
    (...args) => ({ ...vi.mocked(useRoute).getMockImplementation()!(...args), path: '/mocked' }),
  )

  const wrapper = await mountSuspended(MyComponent)
  expect(wrapper.find('#path').text()).toBe('/mocked')
})
```

##### `registerEndpoint` improvements

`registerEndpoint` now works correctly with query parameters in URLs
([#​1560](https://redirect.github.com/nuxt/test-utils/pull/1560)),
and endpoints registered in setup files are no longer lost when modules
reset
([#​1549](https://redirect.github.com/nuxt/test-utils/pull/1549)).

#### 🚧 Migration

`@nuxt/test-utils` v4 contains a few breaking changes, almost all
related to requiring at least **vitest v4** as a peer dependency (if you
are using vitest). It replaces `vite-node` with Vite's native [Module
Runner](https://vite.dev/guide/api-environment-runtimes.html#modulerunner)
and simplifies pool configuration.

This will mean improvements for test performance and mocking, but does
require some changes to your test code.

> \[!TIP]
> Most of the changes below are straightforward. The biggest thing to
watch out for is code that runs at the top level of a `describe` block —
see [below](#describe-blocks-run-during-collection).

##### Update your dependencies

Update `vitest` and its companion packages together:

```diff
{
  "devDependencies": {
-   "@​nuxt/test-utils": "^3.x",
-   "vitest": "^3.x",
-   "@​vitest/coverage-v8": "^3.x"
+   "@​nuxt/test-utils": "^4.0",
+   "vitest": "^4.0",
+   "@​vitest/coverage-v8": "^4.0"
  }
}
```

##### Peer dependencies

We've tightened peer dependency ranges. You may need to update some of
these:

| Dependency             | v3                      | v4          |
| ---------------------- | ----------------------- | ----------- |
| `vitest`               | `^3.2.0`                | `^4.0.2`    |
| `happy-dom`            | `*`                     | `>=20.0.11` |
| `jsdom`                | `*`                     | `>=27.4.0`  |
| `@jest/globals`        | `^29.5.0 \|\| >=30.0.0` | `>=30.0.0`  |
| `@cucumber/cucumber`   | `^10.3.1 \|\| >=11.0.0` | `>=11.0.0`  |
| `@testing-library/vue` | `^7.0.0 \|\| ^8.0.1`    | `^8.0.1`    |

##### Later environment setup

This is the change that might require most change in your tests. Because
we've moved the nuxt environment setup into `beforeAll`, this means
composables called at the top level of a `describe` block will fail with
`[nuxt] instance unavailable`.

```ts
// Before (worked in vitest v3)
describe('my test', () => {
  const router = useRouter() // ran lazily, after environment setup
  // ...
})

// After (vitest v4)
describe('my test', () => {
  let router: ReturnType<typeof useRouter>

  beforeAll(() => {
    router = useRouter() // runs after environment setup
  })
  // ...
})
```

This applies to `useRouter()`, `useNuxtApp()`, `useRoute()`, and any
other Nuxt composable or auto-import.

> \[!TIP]
> If you only need the value within individual tests, `beforeEach` or
directly within the test works too.

##### Stricter mock exports

If you use `vi.mock` with a factory function, accessing an export that
the factory doesn't return will now **throw an error** instead of
silently returning `undefined`.

```ts
// Before: accessing `bar` would silently return undefined
vi.mock('./module', () => ({ foo: 'mocked' }))

// After: accessing `bar` throws
// Fix: use importOriginal to include all exports
vi.mock('./module', async (importOriginal) => ({
  ...await importOriginal(),
  foo: 'mocked',
}))
```

> \[!NOTE]
> If you're mocking a virtual module (like `#build/nuxt.config.mjs`)
where `importOriginal` can't resolve the real module, you might need to
explicitly list all accessed exports in your mock factory.

##### Other changes

For the full list, see the [vitest v4 migration
guide](https://vitest.dev/guide/migration#vitest-4).

commit.message:
[MrBot] Make /PROFILE linker flag opt-in via use_profile cmake option (#288)

* [MrBot] Make /PROFILE linker flag opt-in via use_profile cmake option

Add a use_profile cmake option (default OFF) to guard the /PROFILE
linker flag on test targets. The /PROFILE flag enables profiling
instrumentation needed by some code coverage tools, but it can cause
severe performance degradation (13x+ slower) when combined with VSTest
code coverage collection on projects with many test DLLs. The overhead
compounds during DLL load/initialization, turning a ~3 minute test run
into a 45+ minute timeout.

Consumers that need /PROFILE for their coverage tooling can enable it
with -Duse_profile:bool=ON in their cmake configuration.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
